feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Humboldt Public Health Officials Confirm Local Whooping Cough Case, Warn of Potential Exposures in Arcata, McKinleyville and Fortuna
entry.summary: Most people think of pertussis, commonly known as whooping cough, as a disease from the past. Unfortunately, it is still a problem and tends to flare up every three to seven years in communities. Right now, staff from the Public Health Branch of the Departmen…

entry.title: Cervical cancer deaths fall to zero in young women given vaccine
entry.summary: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

entry.title: Hunter-gatherer cemeteries reveal an ancient plague’s earliest known victims
entry.summary: Hunter-gatherer communities, especially young children, were hit hard by an early strain of plague 5,500 years ago.

entry.title: Cortical development dynamics across autism spectrum disorder mouse models
entry.summary: Using single-nucleus multi-omic sequencing, diverse autism spectrum disorder-linked gene mutations converge on transient, stage-specific disruptions in early brain development, and highlight sex-specific gene expression alterations.

entry.title: New Research Pinpoints Hidden Brain Trigger That Causes Autism, Changing How We Understand The Condition
entry.summary: “The autism diagnosis has dramatically increased. It's alarming,” a netizen said.

entry.title: Binge-eating mystery affecting millions of women may finally be solved: study
entry.summary: Researchers identify a medical connection tied to binge eating in women, shedding light on cravings and eating pattern behaviors.

entry.title: This Deadly and Entirely Preventable Disease Is On the Rise. Here's How You Can Stay Safe
entry.summary: According to CDC data, tetanus cases are increasing in the United States. Doctors explain what's going on and how you can protect yourself.

entry.title: The Parents at My Kid’s School Have the Same Reaction When I Mention My Girlfriend. It’s Been a Year—Get Over It!
entry.summary: She just laughs it off.

entry.title: California resident infected with rare tick-borne illness, officials confirm
entry.summary: The CDPH confirmed a case of Rickettsia lanei, similar to Rocky Mountain spotted fever, in a Northern California patient diagnosed in April.

entry.title: 'Lightning Crotch' Is A Pregnancy Symptom Not Enough People Talk About
entry.summary: The name is memorable for a reason. Here's what's actually behind this surprisingly common experience.

entry.title: Oldest evidence of a plague outbreak found in prehistoric graves, rewriting the disease’s history
entry.summary: A new study describes the oldest evidence of a plague outbreak ever found, in a set of skeletons excavated from prehistoric graves in Siberia dating back 5,500 years.

entry.title: Dangerous tapeworm linked to severe liver disease spreads into Washington coyotes, researchers report
entry.summary: Signs may take five to 15 years to show up, which can make diagnosis difficult.

entry.title: Avocado vs. Hummus: Which Is Better for the Mediterranean Diet?
entry.summary: Dietitians compare avocado and hummus and explain both how these foods fit into the Mediterranean diet.

entry.title: Why just sharing a stick while walking can significantly improve balance
entry.summary: Could the secret to preventing dangerous falls be surprisingly simple? For older adults, these unexpected tumbles are a leading cause of injury, affecting roughly 1 in 4 people 65 and older each year. What if the key isn't complex technology or intensive ther…

entry.title: Ozempic users may be making a major weight-loss mistake, new study suggests
entry.summary: New research finds GLP-1 users significantly reduced physical activity, with daily steps and exercise declining after starting a weight loss medication.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Uses the Best Trick He Knows To Calm Crying Baby
entry.summary: Fussy infants usually have a team of exhausted parents trying absolutely everything to get them back to sleep. However, an incredibly sweet canine decided to...

entry.title: 5 ways to boost your gut health by eating more fermented foods
entry.summary: Scientists have found that eating fermented foods may lower inflammation, improve blood sugar control and increase the diversity of your gut microbiome.

entry.title: Kennedy orders American exposed to hantavirus to stay quarantined against her will, WSJ reports
entry.summary: has ordered an American passenger exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship to remain in quarantine despite medical advice and against her ‌will, the Wall Street...

entry.title: 'I felt trapped': The hidden struggle of eating disorders facing pregnant women
entry.summary: Pregnancy can be a "perfect storm" for eating disorders that is often overlooked.

entry.title: You really need to be thinking more about your skeleton — before it’s too late
entry.summary: Building a strong skeleton is important for protecting organs, storing essential minerals and, quite literally, keeping you upright.

entry.title: An experimental fentanyl vaccine showed promise in an early-stage trial
entry.summary: The vaccine could one day be used to help prevent overdoses and treat addiction.

entry.title: As a physician, I have never been more concerned about rates of congenital syphilis
entry.summary: “As a physician and former public health official, I have never been more concerned about those rates of congenital syphilis,” writes Jeffrey D. Klausner.

entry.title: Scientists Find Intriguing Link Between Ozempic and Violent Behavior
entry.summary: The same mechanisms that dampen people's cravings for food might also affect our tendency for violent behavior, new research suggests.

entry.title: Hantavirus cruise passenger says she’s being forced to quarantine in Nebraska
entry.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said her isolation was needed to protect public health, though the CDC has recommended home quarantine under certain conditions.

entry.title: Local man critically ill after rare tick bite
entry.summary: A New Hampshire man is in critical condition after a tick bite led to a rare Powassan virus infection.

entry.title: Hair restoration options offer new hope for patients experiencing hair loss
entry.summary: Hair loss can happen to anyone, but thanks to advancements in treatment and technology, patients today have more options than ever before to preserve, restore,

entry.title: Scientists Reveal Why Mosquitoes Bite Some People More Than Others
entry.summary: Ever felt like mosquitoes bite you while ignoring everyone else? Scientists are now making progress in deciphering the complex chemical cocktail that makes particular people more enticing to these disease-spreading bloodsuckers.

entry.title: The Best Time to Take Vitamin D for Maximum Absorption, According to Health Experts
entry.summary: Should you take your vitamin D supplement in the morning or at night? Learn what health experts say about when it's best to get the most benefits.

entry.title: 4 Late-Night Drinks for Better Blood Sugar, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Hydration is helpful for balancing blood sugar, but not all drinks are equal. Dietitians share the four best evening drinks for healthy blood sugar.

entry.title: Several men diagnosed with rare infection after frequenting the same gay bathhouse
entry.summary: Human-to-human sexual transmission of the virus had not been seen until now.

entry.title: A three-minute smartphone game can detect a subtle cognitive mechanism behind depression
entry.summary: People with depression experience a measurable shift in their expectations for reward, making pleasure harder to achieve. A new study in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences finds a simple game can quickly detect this change.

entry.title: Cockroaches scurry around with thousands of pieces of bacterial genomes
entry.summary: Transferring genes across species doesn't just happen in microbes.

entry.title: Man hospitalized for weeks with tick-borne illness. What is Powassan virus?
entry.summary: What to know about Powassan virus after a man has been hospitalized with the serious tick-borne illness.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo could become worst in history, Africa CDC warns
entry.summary: The ⁠number of confirmed cases in ⁠the country has increased to 837, including 196 deaths.

entry.title: Northern California resident infected with extremely rare tick-borne illness
entry.summary: A Northern California resident was hospitalized with Rickettsia lanei, a newly recognized tick-borne bacterium linked to severe illness.

entry.title: Taking laxatives can help with memory and attention span problems: study
entry.summary: A new study out Monday may have found a way to flush the brain fog and attention issues that accompany depression down the toilet.

entry.title: Jamestown Canyon virus detected in Grand Rapids mosquito
entry.summary: The Kent County Health Department says at least one mosquito collected from western Grand Rapids has tested positive for Jamestown Canyon virus.

entry.title: AI and brain-computer interface allow speechless ALS patient to work a full-time job
entry.summary: The hardware isn't new, but a UC Davis research team's machine learning-powered method of translating brain activity in an ALS patient into sentences with 92% accuracy is

entry.title: ‘In Defense of Sunlight’ Review: A Star Is Scorned
entry.summary: The media is saturated with dire findings about the risks of sun exposure. Lost in the noise are the undeniable health benefits of catching rays.

entry.title: One Brain Chemical May Be Key to Breaking a Habit, Study Finds
entry.summary: How do you handle disappointment? Are you sometimes defiant, doubling down in the face of failure, or do you typically adapt with aplomb? According to a new study involving mice, the behavioral flexibility behind our reactions to disappointment may be reliant…

entry.title: Non-Statin Ways To Lower Your Cholesterol That Doctors Actually Recommend
entry.summary: If your last cholesterol check came back higher than you'd hoped, take a deep breath—you have options. Plenty of women want to try something other than medic...

entry.title: How intermittent fasting may shield the brain from chronic stress
entry.summary: Chronic stress, the prolonged exposure to psychological and/or physical strain, is known to be a risk factor for depression, anxiety and some other psychiatric disorders. Past studies suggest that chronic stress disrupts the integrity of myelin, a fatty insul…

entry.title: Two simple habits may outperform a popular longevity wonder drug: study
entry.summary: A buzzy diabetes drug that’s been hailed as a longevity booster may have some competition.

entry.title: Weight loss drugs slash risk of 4 types of cancer by 50% or more, study finds
entry.summary: Scientists have found that GLP-1 medications, such as Ozempic, and Wegovy, is linked to a decrease in the overall risk of developing obesity-related cancers,...

entry.title: DRC’s Ebola outbreak may be worst ever, Africa CDC says
entry.summary: The head of Africa’s Centres for Disease ​Control and Prevention warned on ‌Tuesday that the Ebola outbreak in Congo could be the ​worst ever, saying that ​currently tens of thousands of ⁠contacts of those ill ​with the disease had not ​been traced.

entry.title: Live with a partner? You may be sharing more microbes than you think
entry.summary: A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.

entry.title: Your Diet Could Be Missing the Key Ingredient for Heart Protection
entry.summary: New research indicates that when it comes to heart health, the types of fruits and vegetables consumed may be just as important as the amount.

entry.title: ‘Sad nipple syndrome’ is leaving women distressed — docs explain what’s going on
entry.summary: It’s when the nipple gets brushed, by someone or something, and a wave of intense negative emotion washes over. Descriptions range from “the deepest pit in my stomach” to upset, d…

entry.title: Our Brains Harbor a Powerful 'Grammatical Engine'
entry.summary: Scans show brain uses a shared system to handle 2 spoken languages

entry.title: Happy Birthday, Sara Beth: Clayton Girl Continues Inspiring Others Through Cancer Journey
entry.summary: CLAYTON, N.C. — When yearbook signing day arrived at LifeSpring Academy, Sara Beth Bagley’s mother expected to see students exchanging signatures and saying goodbye for the summer. One of the school’s traditions is gathering all students – from kindergarten t…
